old_claim.note: Technical Drawing Standards provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Applications had to include drawings and written references where the invention admitted drawings, so technical drawing conventions enabled examiners to inspect and compare inventions.

source_shape.source_locator: Section 6 requires drawings and written references where the invention admits drawings, and Section 7 makes the filed application the subject of examination.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source shows that drawings were part of patent applications examined under the 1836 system.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This upgrades the edge because technical drawing standards help make application drawings legible for examination, while the statute does not make drawing conventions the sole legal foundation.

invariant_preserved_or_changed: Preserved: technical_drawing_standards remains an enabling edge; changed: the edge now has primary-source evidence and a patent-application-specific note.
would_reject_if: The node were narrowed to legal-only examination that explicitly excludes drawings and models.
A source showed drawings were not used in the 1836 professional examination process.
The technical_drawing_standards node were narrowed to non-technical art conventions only.
short_reason: The 1836 Act directly required application drawings where applicable.
